extra.reasoning: The property requires NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) for all inputs. rec_drug_a is set true only by rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50): IF (elderly AND pregnancy). rec_drug_b is set true only by rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50): IF elderly. With elderly=true and pregnancy=true, both antecedents are satisfied: rec_a_from_conjunction fires making rec_drug_a=true (it is the sole rule for output a, so it wins and is not overridden by the default), and rec_b_from_trigger fires making rec_drug_b=true (sole rule for output b). Priorities and tie-breaking are irrelevant here since each output has exactly one matching rule. The remaining inputs (qt_prolongation, bradycardia, polypharmacy) only affect rec_drug_c, so they are set false for a minimal witness; rec_drug_c stays false via default. Thus rec_drug_a=true and rec_drug_b=true simultaneously, jointly true, violating mutual exclusion.
